# Magistrala

> Open-source IoT platform for cloud and edge — built by Abstract Machines SAS (Paris, France)

Magistrala is Apache 2.0 licensed middleware for constructing complex IoT applications.
It supports MQTT, CoAP, HTTP, and WebSocket protocols with multi-tenancy, RBAC via SpiceDB,
and pluggable storage backends (PostgreSQL, InfluxDB, MongoDB, Redis).

Also known as SuperMQ (the underlying engine). Maintained by Abstract Machines SAS.

## Documentation

- [Overview](https://magistrala.absmach.eu/docs): Platform introduction and architecture
- [Getting Started](https://magistrala.absmach.eu/docs/dev-guide/getting-started): Quick start guide
- [User Guide](https://magistrala.absmach.eu/docs/user-guide): Features and usage
- [API Reference](https://magistrala.absmach.eu/docs/dev-guide/api): REST API documentation
- [Full LLM Docs](https://magistrala.absmach.eu/llms-full.txt): Complete documentation in plain text

## Key Features

- Multi-protocol messaging: MQTT, CoAP, HTTP, WebSocket
- Fine-grained RBAC via SpiceDB (Google Zanzibar model)
- Multi-tenancy with domain isolation
- Pluggable message brokers: NATS, Kafka, RabbitMQ
- Docker and Kubernetes ready
- Free tier + Enterprise plans

## Source

- GitHub: https://github.com/absmach/magistrala
- License: Apache 2.0
- Company: Abstract Machines SAS, 141 Quai de Valmy, Paris 75010, France
